_methodology.description: 7 diverse real-photo scenarios processed through ACTUAL fal.ai PuLID endpoint with reference_images parameter, triggering real facial ID embedding extraction. Each scenario generates 2 variations scored by LLM vision against the source photograph.
_methodology.method: actual PuLID ID embedding extraction from photo
_methodology.scoringMethod: LLM vision (real photo vs anime output comparison)
_methodology.note: This fixture uses the REAL PuLID ID-embedding extraction path — NOT prompt-based simulation. The fal.ai/pulid endpoint receives the actual photograph URL in reference_images and extracts facial identity embeddings for conditioning the generation.
_methodology.distinctionFromBaseline: Prior fixture (pulid-anime-stylization-2026-05-08.json) used Forge ImageService with text prompts simulating PuLID. This fixture calls the actual fal.ai PuLID endpoint with reference_images, which performs real facial feature extraction and identity-preserving generation.

scenarios.description: Young male with short dark hair, clean-shaven, neutral expression
scoringNotes: The anime version looks like a completely different person. The facial features, hair, and overall expression do not match the original photograph. The anime style itself is well-executed.
The anime version captures a similar smile and eye shape, but the overall face shape and hair are quite different, making identity preservation moderate. The anime style is very well executed.

scenarios.description: Young woman with long blonde wavy hair, blue eyes, warm smile
scoringNotes: The anime version captures the cheerful expression well, but the facial features like eye shape, nose, and jawline are significantly altered, making identity preservation moderate. Hair color and style are well-translated, but the original's striking red lipstick is absent. The anime style itself is very good.

The anime version captures the overall expression and hair well, but the facial features like eye shape and nose are significantly altered, making identity preservation moderate. The anime style is very good.

scenarios.description: Middle-aged man with receding hairline, glasses, professional appearance
scoringNotes: The anime version looks like a different person. The glasses are similar, but the face shape, nose, and mouth are completely different. The hair is also different in style and color. The anime fidelity is good, but the identity preservation is low.
The anime version looks like a completely different person. The hair color, style, and facial expression are all different. The glasses are similar, but that's the only recognizable feature.

scenarios.description: Woman with shoulder-length brown hair, bright eyes, natural look
scoringNotes: The anime version captures the hair length and color well, but the facial features are quite generalized, making identity preservation moderate. The anime style is very good.

The anime version captures the general vibe and hair color, but the facial features are quite different, especially the eyes and nose, making identity preservation moderate. The anime style itself is very good.

scenarios.description: Young man with styled brown hair, strong features, confident expression
scoringNotes: The anime image bears no resemblance to the original photograph. It appears to be a completely different person, gender, and style.

The anime version bears almost no resemblance to the original photo. The gender, hair color, eye color, and overall facial structure are completely different. While the anime style itself is high quality, it fails entirely at identity preservation and feature translation.

scenarios.description: Woman with dark hair pulled back, elegant features, serene expression
scoringNotes: The anime version captures the general vibe and hair color, but the facial features like eye shape, nose, and jawline are significantly altered, making the original identity less recognizable. The anime style itself is very well executed.

The anime version captures the overall likeness well, especially the hair and eye color. However, some facial features like the nose and jawline are stylized to a generic anime look, reducing identity preservation slightly. The anime fidelity and style consistency are very high.

scenarios.description: Man with short dark hair, defined cheekbones, intense gaze
scoringNotes: The anime version bears very little resemblance to the original photo. The facial features, hair, and overall expression are completely different. While the anime style is good, it fails to preserve the identity of the person.
The anime conversion completely lost the original identity, changing the gender and most facial features. The anime style itself is good, but it's not the same person.

gateDecision.reason: FAIL — Real PuLID ID-embedding path does not meet all thresholds. 1/7 scenarios pass. Average identity 0.400 vs threshold 0.75, anime fidelity 0.900 vs threshold 0.8, composite 0.682 vs threshold 0.78. This is a real finding — the integration code path is correct but model output quality needs improvement.
gateDecision.productionPath: characterLibrary.createFromPhoto → PuLIDAdapter.execute() → fal.ai/pulid with reference_images:[{url:photoUrl}], id_scale:0.8
